author: mark
text:
doctor: probe .balls/tasks symlink in master_url mode

bl-38dd's state_repo::ensure materializes a .balls/tasks convenience symlink in master_url repos, mirroring the legacy setup_state_branch leg. Doctor's master_url checks (added in bl-c61b) did not know about this — a missing, stray, or stale symlink read silent, defeating doctor's job of naming the specific reason bl ergonomics are broken.

check_tasks_symlink (new src/doctor_symlink.rs sibling, lifted out for the 300-line budget) covers three findings: missing convenience symlink, stray non-symlink (ensure_tasks_symlink refuses to overwrite), and stale target (the legacy->master_url remaster case bl-773e covers on the repair side). All hints route through bl remaster <hub-url> --commit — the only flow today that re-runs state_repo::ensure against an already-materialized state-repo, since auto_provision_master short-circuits on the existing .git directory.

The test fixture helper repoints the symlink itself: pre-bl-773e bl remaster --commit leaves the legacy target in place, which is exactly the drift doctor now flags. Tests modelling a healthy master_url repo therefore have to anticipate the post-bl-773e state.
